subject: Re: Error with Reconstructing Sub-Tomograms at Binned Pixel Size
body:
Hi Thomas,

At 3.2 A Nyquist and the high defocus values tilt series often have, the 
reconstruction procedure can go crazy trying to increase the box size to 
include all delocalized signal. 1.1.0 goes less crazy in general, but you 
can also tick the "use CPU for some steps" checkbox. This should help if 
the problem is what I think it is. However, I'm clueless why it tries to 
export 150k particles based on a 12k file.

Cheers,
Dimitry

On Friday, September 16, 2022 at 7:54:02 PM UTC+5 tn3w...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hello,
>
> I have switched over to Relion 3.1 instead of Relion 4.0, re-ran the 3D 
> auto-refine in Relion, and am still having the same errors after 
> downgrading the file from 3.1 to 3.0 and bringing it into Warp to 
> reconstruct at 1.6Å/px. 
>
> Any advice on this would be great.
>
> Thanks,
> Thomas
>
> On Thursday, September 15, 2022 at 8:18:38 AM UTC-4 lorenzo...@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Hi Tommy,
>>
>> There currently isn't a quick conversion tool from 4.0 to 3.0 (nor 3.1) 
>> that I know of; I definitely plan to make one at some point and add it to 
>> the guide, but I don't really have the time right now. I'm happy to help if 
>> you want to give it a shot, though :) You can take a look at the 3.1>3.0 
>> source code for inspiration: 
>> https://github.com/alisterburt/dynamo2m/blob/master/dynamo2m/relion_star_downgrade.py
>>
>> But Yeah, I agree with the above that currently your best bet is to work 
>> with 3.1 or 3.0. Also, note that a new warp beta (
>> https://www.dropbox.com/s/9w59sdwawo32ux2/Warp_110_beta1.zip?dl=0) just 
>> came out and it should handle 3.1 directly, which saves you from having to 
>> do this conversion.
>>
>> Best,
>> Lorenzo
>>
>> On Wed, Sep 14, 2022 at 11:07 PM Tommy Newton <tn3w...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Pranav,
>>>
>>> Relion 4 is just the version that the facility I am working with had 
>>> universally switched over to. To be honest with you, I only started last 
>>> week, so I am just using the workstation that they have set up. 
>>>
>>> If I am able to switch over and use 3.1 rather than 4, I probably will 
>>> not run into any trouble. However, if that is not an option, do you know of 
>>> any solutions to the problems?
>>>
>>> Thanks,
>>> Thomas
>>>
>>> On Wednesday, September 14, 2022 at 5:00:02 PM UTC-4 p.sha...@gmail.com 
>>> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Thomas, 
>>>> You are likely to have more success with using relion 3.1 than 4.0. Is 
>>>> there any particular reason for choosing 4 over 3.1? The utility of 
>>>> relion in this pipeline is to only find the optimal poses of the 
>>>> particles while M does most of the heavy lifting in terms of 
>>>> corrections. As such using even older versions of relion (such 3.0) 
>>>> will be sufficient. 
>>>>
>>>> TL;DR - Dont actively make the problem harder for yourself when 
>>>> simpler solutions exist ;) 
>>>> Best, 
>>>> Pranav 
>>>> -- 
>>>> Pranav Shah 
>>>> Postdoctoral Research Fellow. 
>>>>
>>>> Division of Structural Biology, 
>>>> Wellcome Trust Centre for Human Genetics, 
>>>> University of Oxford, 
>>>> Roosevelt Drive, Oxford OX3 7BN, 
>>>> UK 
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> On Wed, Sep 14, 2022 at 9:52 PM Tommy Newton <tn3w...@gmail.com> 
>>>> wrote: 
>>>> > 
>>>> > Hello, 
>>>> > 
>>>> > I have been working on the TeamTomo tutorial and have run into 
>>>> trouble in the "Particle Pose Optimization" section. 
>>>> > 
>>>> > I was successfully able to run the 3D auto-refine in Relion. However, 
>>>> I am using Relion 4.0, not Relion 3.1 as the tutorial was written for. I 
>>>> ran the command below to convert the file: 
>>>> > 
>>>> > relion_star_downgrade -s run_it023_data.star 
>>>> > 
>>>> > When moving over to Warp to reconstruct the sub-tomograms at 1.6Å/px, 
>>>> I selected this file and entered the parameters as displayed on the 
>>>> tutorial. The system that I am using runs on two GPUs, so the 
>>>> reconstruction process opened up terminals for both GPUs to track the 
>>>> process. 
>>>> > 
>>>> > The first problem is that the terminal reads out that there are over 
>>>> 148,000 particles needing to be exported, despite the .star file only 
>>>> showing that there are around 12,500 particles to be used. The second 
>>>> problem is that Warp crashes after about 5 minutes of processing. The error 
>>>> begins with "System.Exception: Couldn't export the particles!" One of the 
>>>> terminals showed that the GPU was out of memory, but that was not true 
>>>> after looking into the system. The error that showed in that terminal was 
>>>> along the lines of what Fadwa experienced in a previous forum post from 
>>>> August 2020, starting with "System.Exception: cudaErrorMemoryAllocation," 
>>>> but I had already defined that the memory should not be a problem. 
>>>> > 
>>>> > I first tried to see if the .star files had all the necessary 
>>>> components (i.e, _rlnCoordinateX, Y, etc), comparing it to that of the 
>>>> .star file that was used for the original sub-tomogram reconstruction 
>>>> before 3D auto refine. All necessary factors were contained in the file 
>>>> upon that inspection. 
>>>> > 
>>>> > I then tried to downgrade the file with the relion_convert_star 
>>>> command, but that produced an empty file. 
>>>> > 
>>>> > Finally, I tested the original 
>>>> 'result_10Apx_nodup_neighbourcleaning_data.star' file from the first round 
>>>> of sub-tomogram reconstruction with the parameters at the step for 1.6Å/px, 
>>>> and that failed as well. 
>>>> > 
>>>> > Is there a different means by which I should be converting the Relion 
>>>> 4.0 .star file to a Relion 3.0 .star file? Or is there a problem with the 
>>>> parameters in Warp (I am using 1.0.9 by the way)? 
>>>> > 
>>>> > Thank you, and please let me know if there is any other information 
>>>> that you need me to provide. 
>>>> > 
>>>> > Thomas 
>>>> > 
>>>> > -- 
>>>> > You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>>> Groups "Warp" group. 
>>>> > To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, 
>>>> send an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com. 
>>>> > To view this discussion on the web visit 
>>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/8230ba46-6feb-4176-bff5-59c2bc7a0fdcn%40googlegroups.com. 
>>>>
>>>>

>>> .
